assertions.expectation: Identifies PDB as the most likely/common cause of stuck upgrades
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'Most common cause of stuck drains' for PDBs and calls them '#1 cause of stuck upgrades'

assertions.expectation: Provides kubectl command to check PDBs (kubectl get pdb --all-namespaces or similar)
assertions.reason: Includes 'kubectl get pdb --all-namespaces' command in multiple places

assertions.expectation: Provides commands to identify which pods are blocking the drain
assertions.reason: Includes 'kubectl get pods --all-namespaces --field-selector=status.phase!=Succeeded -o wide | grep <old-node-names>' command to identify problematic pods

assertions.expectation: Includes resource constraint diagnosis (capacity issues, pending pods)
assertions.reason: Response does not address resource constraints, capacity issues, or pending pods as potential causes

assertions.expectation: Provides a fix for restrictive PDBs (patch or temporary adjustment)
assertions.reason: Provides kubectl patch commands to adjust minAvailable to 0 and increase maxUnavailable for PDBs

assertions.expectation: Includes guidance on surge upgrade settings as a potential fix
assertions.reason: Shows gcloud commands to set max-surge and max-unavailable parameters for upgrade strategy

assertions.expectation: Mentions webhook issues as a possible cause
assertions.reason: Response does not mention admission controllers, validating webhooks, or webhook-related issues

assertions.expectation: Includes validation steps to confirm the fix is working
assertions.reason: Includes 'Monitor Progress' section with watch commands to monitor upgrade progress and pod rescheduling
